heading: Disclosure of predictive modeling and other analytics technologies to identify and prevent waste, fraud, and abuse
label: 1320a–7n Disclosure of predictive modeling and other analytics technologies to identify and prevent waste, fraud, and abuse

children.heading: Reference to predictive modeling technologies requirements
children.label: a Reference to predictive modeling technologies requirements
children.text: For provisions relating to the use of predictive modeling and other analytics technologies to identify and prevent waste, fraud, and abuse with respect to the Medicare program under subchapter XVIII, the Medicaid program under subchapter XIX, and the Children’s Health Insurance Program under subchapter XXI, see .section 1320a–7m of this title

children.heading: Limiting disclosure of predictive modeling technologies
children.label: b Limiting disclosure of predictive modeling technologies

children.heading: Nonapplication of FOIA
children.label: 1 Nonapplication of FOIA
children.text: The covered algorithms used or developed for purposes of such section 1320a–7m (including by the Secretary or a State (or an entity operating under a contract with a State)) shall be exempt from disclosure under .section 552(b)(3) of title 5

children.heading: Limitation with respect to use and disclosure of information by State agencies
children.label: 2 Limitation with respect to use and disclosure of information by State agencies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State agency may not use or disclose covered algorithms used or developed for purposes of such section 1320a–7m except for purposes of administering the State plan (or a waiver of the plan) under the Medicaid program under subchapter XIX or the State child health plan (or a waiver of the plan) under the Children’s Health Insurance Program under subchapter XXI, including by enabling an entity operating under a contract with a State to assist the State to identify or prevent waste, fraud, and abuse with respect to such programs.

children.heading: Information security
children.label: B Information security
children.text: A State agency shall have in effect data security and control policies that the Secretary finds adequate to ensure the security of covered algorithms used or developed for purposes of such section 1320a–7m and to ensure that access to such information is restricted to authorized persons for purposes of authorized uses and disclosures described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Procedural requirements
children.label: C Procedural requirements
children.text: State agencies to which information is disclosed pursuant to such section 1320a–7m shall adhere to uniform procedures established by the Secretary.

means a predictive modeling or other analytics technology, as used for purposes of to identify and prevent waste, fraud, and abuse with respect to the Medicare program under subchapter XVIII, the Medicaid program under subchapter XIX, and the Children’s Health Insurance Program under subchapter XXI; andsection 1320a–7m(a) of this title
includes the mathematical expressions utilized in the application of such technology and the means by which such technology is developed.
